_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Integrate continuous monitoring results in ongoing authorizations
reference_document_element_description: Implement platform monitoring and alerting for availability, performance, cost, and security events, with defined escalation procedures.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI DA domain. Task DA-T-009 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-T-013 (DG, cosine: 0.452), AI-T-017 (AI, cosine: 0.451).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (5.8/10). The cross-encoder finds meaningful but bounded overlap — consistent with cross-framework mapping between cybersecurity and data/AI domains where shared governance concepts use different terminology.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: Moderate token-level alignment (F1=0.862). Balanced P/R (0.867/0.857) indicating symmetric token-level overlap.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (100.0%). The model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other — typical for cross-domain framework mapping where concepts overlap without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder (0.454) trails STS (0.582). Cross-attention captures semantic relationships that independent encoding misses — typical for cross-domain pairs where shared meaning exists beneath divergent vocabulary.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Near-zero lexical overlap (4.8%). The concepts use almost entirely different vocabulary, confirming the relationship is detected through semantic understanding, not word matching.
